question: Comment configurer les permissions et le sandbox pour limiter le blast radius d'un agent IA ?

principles.text: Appliquer le principe du least-privilege avec deny-by-default + allow-list explicite des outils accessibles. Combiner avec isolation au niveau OS : microVM (E2B/Firecracker, Devin VM, Sculptor par Imbue) pour isolation hardware ; devcontainers ou git-worktrees pour isolation moindre mais plus ergonomique. Le shared-kernel container seul est insuffisant pour du code AI non-trusted. Les regles deny doivent avoir precedence sur allow. Ne jamais utiliser 'bypassPermissions' ou YOLO mode contre des repos production (prompt injection documente peut exfiltrer secrets / executer rm -rf).

sources.name: Claude Code permissions documentation
sources.quote: Rules are evaluated in order: deny -> ask -> allow. The first matching rule wins, so deny rules always take precedence

sources.name: NIST AI 600-1 §2.10 Information Security risk category
ISO/IEC 23894:2023 - Guidance on AI risk management
sources.quote: This document provides guidance on how organizations that develop, produce, deploy or use products, systems and services that utilize artificial intelligence (AI) can manage risk specifically related to AI

sources.name: E2B Firecracker microVMs documentation
sources.quote: Built on Firecracker microVMs for full isolation

sources.name: Claude Code issue #10077 - rm -rf incident in bypass mode

etd_balance.benefices: Isolation incidents destructifs (rm -rf case), conformite ISO 42001 + NIST
